_note: Stage-3 production drills. Each item: a content skeleton (cues separated by /) + a [trigger] word lifted from that function's OWN cue cards (so completing it requires retrieving that specific drilled frame). Student assembles the full sentence deploying the trigger; then reveals model answer(s), which show valid alternative frames where the trigger lives in more than one construction. Keyed by function id.

models: A major advantage of electric cars is that they produce no emissions.
One of the primary strengths of online learning is that it lets you study anywhere.
The greatest value of public transport lies in the fact that it reduces traffic.
A notable upside of learning a language is that it opens doors.

advantages.cue: working from home / save / commuting time
models: A significant feature of working from home is that it saves commuting time.
A core strength of regular exercise is that it improves your health.
A major drawback of social media is that it can harm mental health.

disadvantages.cue: living in a city / cost / a lot
models: The biggest downside of living in a city is that it costs a lot.
A notable disadvantage of studying abroad is that it separates you from family.

disadvantages.cue: relying on technology / weaken / real skills
models: A significant weakness of relying on technology is that it weakens real skills.
One of the key concerns with fast food is that it damages health.
One of the main challenges associated with long working hours is that they cause stress.
One of the key problems with heavy traffic is that it pollutes cities.
One of the main issues with social media is that it spreads false information.
One of the primary concerns surrounding mass tourism is that it overwhelms small towns.
One of the central difficulties associated with exam pressure is that it affects wellbeing.
One of the most significant challenges facing us is that pollution threatens public health.
One of the critical weaknesses of modern habits is that screen addiction reduces focus.
One way to address this issue is to build more cycle lanes.
One effective approach is to make lessons more interactive.
A possible solution would be to tax sugary drinks.
One practical step would be to offer flexible hours.
A useful strategy is to raise public awareness.
An important way to overcome this challenge is to reduce plastic use.
This is mainly caused by poor diet.
This can largely be attributed to young people leaving.
This issue often stems from a heavy workload.
This is primarily the result of a lack of practice.
A major contributing factor is the number of cars on the road.
The root cause of this is the sheer convenience of online shopping.
The obvious impact of pollution is that it harms people's health.
One major consequence of remote work is that it blurs work-life balance.

effect.cue: social media / change / how we communicate
models: A significant effect of social media is that it changes how we communicate.
This often results in rising rents.
This can lead to poor sleep.
This has a direct influence on the prices locals pay.
This is because cities offer more jobs.
The main reason for this is that students prefer practical subjects.
This can be explained by the fact that texting is cheaper than calling.
This is largely due to the fact that people value their free time.
This occurs as a result of remote work raising productivity.
This means that travel genuinely broadens the mind.

opinion.cue: social media / do / more harm than good
models: In my opinion, social media does more harm than good.
I believe that studying abroad is worth the cost.
I would argue that technology improves our lives.
In my view, exams do not measure real ability.

opinion.trigger: as far as I am concerned
models: As far as I am concerned, English is essential today.
It seems to me that cities are better for young people.
I completely agree that technology makes life easier.
I strongly support the idea that travel is the best form of education.
There is no doubt that reading improves vocabulary.
I fully agree with the view that exercise benefits everyone.
I firmly believe that languages open opportunities.
I share the belief that cities offer better services.
I completely disagree that social media brings people closer.
I am not convinced that young people are lazier than before.
I strongly oppose the view that exams are the fairest way to test students.
I do not believe that technology makes life meaningless.
I strongly reject the idea that city life is always better.
It is inaccurate to claim that studying abroad is always worth it.

partial.cue: cities / better / but / countryside / has charm
models: I partly agree that cities are better, although the countryside has its charm.
While I agree to some extent, technology can also distract.

partial.cue: exams / useful / but / not the only measure
models: There is some truth in the idea that exams are useful, but they are not the only measure.

partial.cue: social media / risky / but / has benefits
models: I can understand both sides of the argument, since social media is risky but has real benefits.
While this is valid in some cases, studying abroad remains expensive.
I agree in part, but I also believe that specialising early can be limiting.
This may be due to the pressures of social media.
It is possible that remote work will become permanent.
This could potentially lead to cities growing even larger.
It is likely that online news will replace newspapers.
It is reasonable to suppose that English will stay the global language.
It can be assumed that electric cars will dominate the market.
There is a tendency for footballers to earn more than teachers.
Generally speaking, young people get their news online.
By and large, newspapers are losing readers.

generalising.cue: people / believe / what they see on TV
models: In most cases, people believe what they see on TV.
On the whole, city residents earn higher salaries.
As a rule, students prefer practical subjects.

comparing.cue: city life / busier / than / country life
models: In contrast to country life, city life is far busier.
Compared to cars, trains are far greener.

comparing.cue: online learning / flexible / classroom learning / structured
models: Online learning is flexible, whereas classroom learning is more structured.
Books are calming; films, on the other hand, are more vivid.
Unlike TV, social media is interactive.
While cities offer opportunity, the countryside offers peace.
It could well be that electric cars cut pollution significantly.
One possibility is that everyone will work from home.
It is not impossible that exams will disappear one day.
It might be argued that English could lose its dominance.
Conceivably, cities could become too crowded.
It is worth considering that people might spend less time online.
It is highly likely that remote work will become the norm.
In all probability, electric cars will replace petrol cars.
The chances are that cities will keep growing.
There is every likelihood that social media will grow further.
It seems probable that pollution will stay a problem.
It is reasonable to expect that English will remain global.

similarities.cue: city life / country life / offer community
models: Both city life and country life offer a sense of community.
In much the same way as travelling, reading broadens the mind.
Similarly, both TV and social media entertain people.
What online and classroom learning have in common is that both need discipline.
There is a clear parallel between all successful students: they work hard.
Just as buses do, trains carry many people at once.

differences.cue: city life / fast / country life / slow
models: In contrast to country life, city life is fast.

differences.cue: today / connected / fifty years ago / isolated
models: Whereas fifty years ago people were more isolated, today they are highly connected.
The key difference is that online learning is flexible while the classroom is fixed.
Trains are relaxing; planes, on the other hand, are fast.

differences.cue: young people / use tech freely / older people / cautiously
models: Young people use technology freely; conversely, older people are more cautious.
The two differ markedly in that school language is theoretical while abroad it is practical.
There has been a marked rise in people shopping online.
There has been a sharp decline in newspaper sales.
Increasingly, young people are moving to cities.
A growing number of workers are now remote.
Screen time is steadily increasing.
The long-term trend has been towards flexible work.
The vast majority of teenagers use social media daily.
A significant proportion of students study hard.
Only a small minority of people limit their screen time.
A considerable number of commuters prefer public transport.
The overwhelming majority of drivers may soon switch to electric.
Very few people actually read newspapers now.
With the exception of healthcare, most jobs can go remote.
Apart from a few cases, technology tends to help.
Except in cases where the subject is dull, the student enjoys everything.

exceptions.cue: manufacturing / all sectors / adopt flexible work
models: Aside from manufacturing, all sectors are adopting flexible work.
Other than in rare situations, social media is rarely essential.

exceptions.cue: small towns / the trend / holds everywhere
models: Barring a few small towns, the trend holds everywhere.
